status: accepted
date: 2026-06-01
---

# Surface `metric_key` on the `POST /v1/catalog/get_metrics` response for the FE-bridge transitional release

**ID**: `cpt-metric-cat-adr-metric-key-on-wire`

## Table of Contents

<!-- generated by `cypilot toc` -->

## Context and Problem Statement

DESIGN.md §3.3 originally pinned a hard invariant:

> `metric_key` is not surfaced and MUST NOT be reverse-engineered from any other field.

The rationale was layer-boundary hygiene — `metric_key` is the backend-internal `<table_name>.<column_name>` identifier and surfacing it would couple consumers to source-schema naming. Consumers were intended to key everything off `id` (UUIDv7) and render `label`.

That invariant breaks the transitional release for constructorfabric/insight-front#66:

1. The FE today carries hand-authored constants — `BULLET_DEFS`, `IC_KPI_DEFS`, `METRIC_SEMANTICS` — keyed by `metric_key` strings (`task_delivery_bullet_rows.tasks_completed`, `ic_kpis.bugs_fixed`, etc.).
2. To delete those constants safely, the FE has to first prove byte-for-byte parity between catalog-hydrated rendering and the legacy compile-in rendering (PRD §12 risk mitigation — "hold the frontend-delete step until comparison passes").
3. The byte-for-byte gate requires a way to align each FE constant entry to the corresponding wire row. Without `metric_key` on the wire there is no determinate alignment — the FE can't say "this `BULLET_DEFS[i]` corresponds to wire row `metrics[j]`" without parsing labels, which is locale-fragile and re-introduces drift.
4. After the constants are deleted, `metric_key` is still useful as a stable join key for debugging ("which catalog row backs `ic_kpis.tasks_closed` in this tenant?") without forcing operators to look up `id` UUIDs by hand.

The original "never surface" rule was conservative — it was written when the FE consumed catalog data through a different mechanism. With the FE-hydration migration in flight, the cost of withholding `metric_key` is concrete (no clean transitional path) while the cost of surfacing it is abstract (consumers *might* couple to source-schema naming).

## Decision Drivers

- The FE needs a determinate bridge between its compile-in constants and wire rows for the byte-for-byte parity gate. Locale-fragile label matching is not acceptable.
- The stable lookup contract — `id` is the wire identifier — MUST be preserved. Surfacing `metric_key` is additive; it doesn't replace `id`.
- The breaking-change policy (PRD §7.1) lets the response add optional fields without a major bump. Surfacing `metric_key` is a textbook additive change.
- The future "FE-controlled rename of `metric_key`" risk (the original justification for hiding it) is bounded: `metric_key` is governed by backend seed migrations; FE engineers don't write it. If the backend ever renames a `metric_key`, it's a coordinated migration regardless of whether consumers see the string or not.

## Considered Options

- **Option A — Surface `metric_key` as a required field on every `metrics[i]` row.**
- **Option B — Add a separate `POST /catalog/get_metric_keys` endpoint** that returns just `{ id, metric_key }` pairs.
- **Option C — Keep `metric_key` hidden** and have the FE align constants to wire rows by label-matching with locale-aware fallback.
- **Option D — Surface `metric_key` only when an opt-in body flag is set** (e.g., `{ include_metric_key: true }`).

## Decision Outcome

Chosen option: **A — surface `metric_key` as a required field on every `metrics[i]` row.**

The hard "never surface" rule in DESIGN.md §3.3 is narrowed to: *"`id` (UUIDv7) is the stable wire lookup identifier and consumers MUST key metadata lookups by `id`. `metric_key` is additionally surfaced as the FE-bridge identifier and as a debugging join key; consumers SHOULD NOT use `metric_key` as their primary lookup key — that contract belongs to `id`."*

The `MetricView` Rust struct in `domain/catalog/response.rs` adds `pub metric_key: String` (required, not `Option`). The seed migration already pins `metric_key` as `NOT NULL` on every `metric_catalog` row, so a missing wire value would be a DB-shape regression — there's no honest case for `Option`.

Per the additive-field stability contract, the field can be added without a major version bump. Existing consumers that don't read `metric_key` are unaffected.

### Consequences

- Good, because the FE byte-for-byte parity gate now has a clean, deterministic bridge — no label-matching, no locale fragility.
- Good, because debugging is easier — `metric_key` is human-meaningful (`ic_kpis.tasks_closed` reads as "the tasks-closed column on the ic_kpis storage table") in a way that UUIDv7 isn't.
- Good, because the change is additive — every existing consumer continues to work; only consumers that opt to read the new field are affected.
- Bad, because future consumers might be tempted to use `metric_key` as a primary lookup key, re-introducing the source-schema coupling the original rule guarded against. Mitigated by:
  - The DESIGN amendment explicitly documents `id` as the stable contract and `metric_key` as the FE-bridge identifier.
  - The Catalog Consumer Contract (DESIGN §3.3) is updated in the same change to spell this out.
- Bad, because reverting this decision (re-hiding `metric_key`) becomes a major-version bump under the response-shape stability contract. Acceptable: by the time this would matter, the FE transitional constants are gone and `metric_key` is purely a debug aid.

### Confirmation

- `cargo check --tests` + `cargo clippy --tests -- -D warnings` are clean.
- `domain/catalog/response::tests::metric_view_serializes_metric_key_for_fe_bridge` pins the field's presence.
- The pre-existing live test `response_never_includes_metric_key_field` is inverted to `response_includes_metric_key_for_fe_bridge` — same gate, opposite expectation.
- DESIGN §3.3 amendment + this ADR ship in the same change as the `MetricView` struct update.

## Pros and Cons of the Options

### Option A — Surface `metric_key` (chosen)

- Good: deterministic FE-bridge identifier; clean transitional path; useful for debugging post-transition.
- Good: additive — no major bump; no consumer breakage.
- Bad: future consumers might over-use it; mitigated by the DESIGN amendment's explicit contract guidance.

### Option B — Separate `POST /catalog/get_metric_keys` endpoint

- Good: keeps the main response surface "pure" (no source-schema name).
- Bad: doubles the cache key space (every consumer now also caches `(tenant_id, metric_keys)`); doubles the request rate during FE hydration.
- Bad: re-introduces the consistency problem — what if `get_metrics` and `get_metric_keys` are served by different replicas with different cache freshness?
- Bad: adds an endpoint that exists only for the transitional release and is awkward to deprecate.

### Option C — Hide `metric_key`, label-match in the FE

- Good: zero backend change; preserves the original "never surface" rule.
- Bad: label-matching is locale-fragile (labels are plain English in v1 but will be localized via the i18n follow-on per PRD §13).
- Bad: re-introduces exactly the FE↔backend drift the metric-catalog initiative exists to eliminate. The FE would carry a label-→-metric_key dictionary that drifts every time a label is reworded.
- Bad: no clean way to do byte-for-byte parity verification — the alignment is approximate, not deterministic.

### Option D — Opt-in body flag

- Good: keeps the response surface "default-pure".
- Bad: doubles the cache key space (cached responses now vary by `include_metric_key` too).
- Bad: server-side cache layer needs a separate key dimension; not a meaningful win over Option A.
- Bad: a body flag that exists only for the transitional release is a wart that's hard to deprecate cleanly.

## More Information

- Wire-shape struct: `src/backend/services/analytics-api/src/domain/catalog/response.rs`
- DESIGN amendment: `docs/domain/metric-catalog/specs/DESIGN.md` §3.3 Catalog Consumer Contract — the "MUST NOT be reverse-engineered" sentence is narrowed.
- FE issue: constructorfabric/insight-front#66 — the consumer that needs this bridge.
- Companion ADR-003 surfaces the `metric_query_catalog` link map for the same transitional release.
